import type { Arrayable, CSSEntry, PreflightContext, PresetOptions } from '@unocss/core' import type { Theme } from './theme' import { definePreset } from '@unocss/core' import { extractorArbitraryVariants } from '@unocss/extractor-arbitrary-variants' import { postprocessors } from './postprocess' import { preflights } from './preflights' import { rules } from './rules' import { shortcuts } from './shortcuts' import { shorthands } from './shorthands' import { theme } from './theme' import { PRESET_NAME, trackedProperties, trackedTheme } from './utils' import { variants } from './variants' export { postprocessors, preflights, rules, shortcuts, shorthands, theme, variants } export type { Theme } export interface DarkModeSelectors { /** * Selector for light variant. * * @default '.light' */ light?: string /** * Selector for dark variant. * * @default '.dark' */ dark?: string } export interface PreflightsTheme { /** * Generate theme keys as CSS variables. * * - `true`: Generate theme keys fully. * - `false`: Disable theme keys. (Not recommended ⚠️) * - `'on-demand'`: Generate theme keys only when used. * * @default 'on-demand' */ mode?: boolean | 'on-demand' /** * Process the theme keys. */ process?: Arrayable<(entry: CSSEntry, ctx: PreflightContext) => void> } export interface PresetWind4Options extends PresetOptions { /** * Dark mode options * * @default 'class' */ dark?: 'class' | 'media' | DarkModeSelectors /** * Generate tagged pseudo selector as `[group=""]` instead of `.group` * * @default false */ attributifyPseudo?: boolean /** * Prefix for CSS variables. * * @default 'un-' */ variablePrefix?: string /** * Utils prefix. When using tagged pseudo selector, only the first truthy prefix will be used. * * @default undefined */ prefix?: string | string[] /** * Enable arbitrary variants, for example `
`. * * Disable this might slightly improve the performance. * * @default true */ arbitraryVariants?: boolean /** * The important option lets you control whether UnoCSS’s utilities should be marked with `!important`. * * This can be really useful when using UnoCSS with existing CSS that has high specificity selectors. * * You can also set `important` to a selector like `#app` instead, which will generate `#app :is(.m-1) { ... }` * * Also check out the compatibility with [:is()](https://caniuse.com/?search=%3Ais()) * * @default false */ important?: boolean | string /** * Control the preflight styles. */ preflights?: { /** * Reset the default preflight styles. * * @default true */ reset?: boolean /** * Theme configuration for preflight styles. * * This can either be a specific mode from `PreflightsTheme['mode']` or a full `PreflightsTheme` object. * * The theme defines the base styles applied to elements and can be customized * to match the design system or requirements of your project. */ theme?: PreflightsTheme['mode'] | PreflightsTheme } } export const presetWind4 = definePreset((options = {}) => { options.dark = options.dark ?? 'class' options.attributifyPseudo = options.attributifyPseudo ?? false options.variablePrefix = options.variablePrefix ?? 'un-' options.important = options.important ?? false const preflightsTheme = (typeof options.preflights?.theme === 'boolean' || typeof options.preflights?.theme === 'string') ? { mode: options.preflights.theme ?? 'on-demand' } : { mode: options.preflights?.theme?.mode ?? 'on-demand', ...options.preflights?.theme } options.preflights = { reset: options.preflights?.reset ?? true, theme: preflightsTheme, } return { name: PRESET_NAME, rules, shortcuts, theme, layers: { properties: -200, theme: -150, base: -100, }, preflights: preflights(options), variants: variants(options), prefix: options.prefix, postprocess: postprocessors(options), extractorDefault: options.arbitraryVariants === false ? undefined : extractorArbitraryVariants(), autocomplete: { shorthands, }, options, configResolved() { trackedTheme.clear() trackedProperties.clear() }, meta: { themeDeps: trackedTheme, propertyDeps: trackedProperties, }, } }) export default presetWind4

     2→import type { Theme } from '../theme'
     3→import { colorableShadows, colorResolver, defineProperty, getStringComponent, globalKeywords, h, isCSSMathFn, numberResolver } from '../utils'
     4→import { bracketTypeRe } from '../utils/handlers/regex'
     5→import { generateThemeVariable, themeTracking } from '../utils/track'
     6→
     7→export const fonts: Rule<Theme>[] = [
     8→  // text
     9→  [/^text-(.+)$/, handleText, { autocomplete: 'text-$text' }],
    10→
    11→  // // text size
    12→  [/^(?:text|font)-size-(.+)$/, handleSize, { autocomplete: 'text-size-$text' }],
    13→
    14→  // text colors
    15→  [/^text-(?:color-)?(.+)$/, handlerColorOrSize, { autocomplete: 'text-$colors' }],
    16→
    17→  // colors
    18→  [/^(?:color|c)-(.+)$/, colorResolver('color', 'text')],
    19→
    20→  // style
    21→  [/^(?:text|color|c)-(.+)$/, ([, v]) => globalKeywords.includes(v) ? { color: v } : undefined, { autocomplete: `(text|color|c)-(${globalKeywords.join('|')})` }],
    22→
    23→  // opacity
    24→  [/^(?:text|color|c)-op(?:acity)?-?(.+)$/, ([, opacity]) => ({ '--un-text-opacity': h.bracket.percent.cssvar(opacity) }), { autocomplete: '(text|color|c)-(op|opacity)-<percent>' }],
    25→
    26→  // weights
    27→  [
    28→    /^fw-?([^-]+)$/,
    29→    ([, s], { theme }) => {
    30→      let v: string | undefined
    31→
    32→      if (theme.fontWeight?.[s]) {
    33→        themeTracking(`fontWeight`, s)
    34→        v = generateThemeVariable('fontWeight', s)
    35→      }
    36→      else {
    37→        v = h.bracket.cssvar.global.number(s)
    38→      }
    39→
    40→      return {
    41→        '--un-font-weight': v,
    42→        'font-weight': v,
    43→      }
    44→    },
    45→    {
    46→      autocomplete: [
    47→        '(font|fw)-(100|200|300|400|500|600|700|800|900)',
    48→        '(font|fw)-$fontWeight',
    49→      ],
    50→    },
    51→  ],
    52→
    53→  // leadings
    54→  [
    55→    /^(?:font-)?(?:leading|lh|line-height)-(.+)$/,
    56→    ([, s], { theme }) => {
    57→      let v: string | undefined
    58→
    59→      if (theme.leading?.[s]) {
    60→        themeTracking('leading', s)
    61→        v = generateThemeVariable('leading', s)
    62→      }
    63→      else if (numberResolver(s)) {
    64→        themeTracking('spacing')
    65→        v = `calc(var(--spacing) * ${numberResolver(s)})`
    66→      }
    67→      else {
    68→        v = h.bracket.cssvar.global.rem(s)
    69→      }
    70→
    71→      if (v != null) {
    72→        return [
    73→          {
    74→            '--un-leading': v,
    75→            'line-height': v,
    76→          },
    77→          defineProperty('--un-leading'),
    78→        ]
    79→      }
    80→    },
    81→    { autocomplete: '(leading|lh|line-height)-$leading' },
    82→  ],
    83→
    84→  // synthesis
    85→  ['font-synthesis-weight', { 'font-synthesis': 'weight' }],
    86→  ['font-synthesis-style', { 'font-synthesis': 'style' }],
    87→  ['font-synthesis-small-caps', { 'font-synthesis': 'small-caps' }],
    88→  ['font-synthesis-none', { 'font-synthesis': 'none' }],
    89→  [/^font-synthesis-(.+)$/, ([, s]) => ({ 'font-synthesis': h.bracket.cssvar.global(s) })],
    90→
    91→  // tracking
    92→  [
    93→    /^(?:font-)?tracking-(.+)$/,
    94→    ([, s], { theme }) => {
    95→      let v: string | undefined
    96→
    97→      if (theme.tracking?.[s]) {
    98→        themeTracking(`tracking`, s)
    99→        v = generateThemeVariable('tracking', s)
   100→      }
   101→      else {
   102→        v = h.bracket.cssvar.global.rem(s)
   103→      }
   104→
   105→      return {
   106→        '--un-tracking': v,
   107→        'letter-spacing': v,
   108→      }
   109→    },
   110→    { autocomplete: 'tracking-$tracking' },
   111→  ],
   112→
   113→  // word-spacing
   114→  [
   115→    /^(?:font-)?word-spacing-(.+)$/,
   116→    ([, s], { theme }) => {
   117→      // Use the same variable as tracking
   118→      const v = theme.tracking?.[s] ? generateThemeVariable('tracking', s) : h.bracket.cssvar.global.rem(s)
   119→      return {
   120→        '--un-word-spacing': v,
   121→        'word-spacing': v,
   122→      }
   123→    },
   124→    { autocomplete: 'word-spacing-$spacing' },
   125→  ],
   126→
   127→  // stretch
   128→  ['font-stretch-normal', { 'font-stretch': 'normal' }],
   129→  ['font-stretch-ultra-condensed', { 'font-stretch': 'ultra-condensed' }],
   130→  ['font-stretch-extra-condensed', { 'font-stretch': 'extra-condensed' }],
   131→  ['font-stretch-condensed', { 'font-stretch': 'condensed' }],
   132→  ['font-stretch-semi-condensed', { 'font-stretch': 'semi-condensed' }],
   133→  ['font-stretch-semi-expanded', { 'font-stretch': 'semi-expanded' }],
   134→  ['font-stretch-expanded', { 'font-stretch': 'expanded' }],
   135→  ['font-stretch-extra-expanded', { 'font-stretch': 'extra-expanded' }],
   136→  ['font-stretch-ultra-expanded', { 'font-stretch': 'ultra-expanded' }],
   137→  [
   138→    /^font-stretch-(.+)$/,
   139→    ([, s]) => ({ 'font-stretch': h.bracket.cssvar.fraction.global(s) }),
   140→    { autocomplete: 'font-stretch-<percentage>' },
   141→  ],
   142→
   143→  // family & weight
   144→  [
   145→    /^font-(.+)$/,
   146→    ([, d], { theme }) => {
   147→      let v: string | undefined
   148→
   149→      // Prefer theme font family
   150→      if (theme.font?.[d]) {
   151→        themeTracking('font', d)
   152→        v = generateThemeVariable('font', d)
   153→        return { 'font-family': v }
   154→      }
   155→
   156→      // Prefer theme font weight
   157→      if (theme.fontWeight?.[d]) {
   158→        themeTracking('fontWeight', d)
   159→        v = generateThemeVariable('fontWeight', d)
   160→        return { '--un-font-weight': v, 'font-weight': v }
   161→      }
   162→
   163→      // Numeric font weight (e.g. font-700)
   164→      v = h.number(d)
   165→      if (v != null) {
   166→        return { '--un-font-weight': v, 'font-weight': v }
   167→      }
   168→
   169→      // Bracketed font family (e.g. font-[family:Inter])
   170→      v = h.bracketOfFamily(d)
   171→      if (v != null && h.number(v) == null) {
   172→        v = h.cssvar(v) ?? v
   173→        return { 'font-family': v }
   174→      }
   175→
   176→      // Bracketed numeric font weight (e.g. font-[number:700])
   177→      v = h.bracketOfNumber(d)
   178→      if (v != null) {
   179→        v = h.cssvar.number(v)
   180→        return { '--un-font-weight': v, 'font-weight': v }
   181→      }
   182→
   183→      // Bracketed value that is a unknown (e.g. font-[sth])
   184→      v = h.bracket(d)
   185→      if (v != null && h.number(v) != null) {
   186→        const num = h.number(v)
   187→        return { '--un-font-weight': num, 'font-weight': num }
   188→      }
   189→
   190→      v = h.bracket.cssvar.global(d)
   191→      if (v != null) {
   192→        return { 'font-family': v }
   193→      }
   194→    },
   195→    { autocomplete: ['font-$font', 'font-$fontWeight'] },
   196→  ],
   197→]
   198→
   199→export const tabSizes: Rule<Theme>[] = [
   200→  [/^tab(?:-(.+))?$/, ([, s]) => {
   201→    const v = h.bracket.cssvar.global.number(s || '4')
   202→    if (v != null) {
   203→      return {
   204→        '-moz-tab-size': v,
   205→        '-o-tab-size': v,
   206→        'tab-size': v,
   207→      }
   208→    }
   209→  }],
   210→]
   211→
   212→export const textIndents: Rule<Theme>[] = [
   213→  [/^indent-(.+)$/, ([, s]) => {
   214→    let v: string | number | undefined = numberResolver(s)
   215→
   216→    if (v != null) {
   217→      themeTracking(`spacing`)
   218→      return { 'text-indent': `calc(var(--spacing) * ${v})` }
   219→    }
   220→
   221→    v = h.bracket.cssvar.auto.global.rem(s)
   222→
   223→    if (v != null) {
   224→      return { 'text-indent': v }
   225→    }
   226→  }],
   227→]
   228→
   229→export const textStrokes: Rule<Theme>[] = [
   230→  // widths
   231→  [/^text-stroke(?:-(.+))?$/, ([, s = 'DEFAULT'], { theme }) => {
   232→    if (theme.textStrokeWidth?.[s]) {
   233→      themeTracking(`textStrokeWidth`, s)
   234→    }
   235→    return {
   236→      '-webkit-text-stroke-width': theme.textStrokeWidth?.[s]
   237→        ? generateThemeVariable('textStrokeWidth', s)
   238→        : h.bracket.cssvar.px(s),
   239→    }
   240→  }, { autocomplete: 'text-stroke-$textStrokeWidth' }],
   241→
   242→  // colors
   243→  [/^text-stroke-(.+)$/, colorResolver('-webkit-text-stroke-color', 'text-stroke'), { autocomplete: 'text-stroke-$colors' }],
   244→  [/^text-stroke-op(?:acity)?-?(.+)$/, ([, opacity]) => ({ '--un-text-stroke-opacity': h.bracket.percent.cssvar(opacity) }), { autocomplete: 'text-stroke-(op|opacity)-<percent>' }],
   245→]
   246→
   247→const opRE = /op(?:acity)?-/
   248→export const textShadows: Rule<Theme>[] = [
   249→  [/^text-shadow(?:-(.+))?$/, (match, ctx) => {
   250→    const [_, s = 'DEFAULT'] = match
   251→    const v = ctx.theme.textShadow?.[s]
   252→    if (v != null) {
   253→      return {
   254→        '--un-text-shadow': colorableShadows(v, '--un-text-shadow-color').join(','),
   255→        'text-shadow': 'var(--un-text-shadow)',
   256→      }
   257→    }
   258→    if (opRE.test(s))
   259→      return { '--un-text-shadow-opacity': h.bracket.percent.cssvar(s.replace(opRE, '')) }
   260→
   261→    return colorResolver('--un-text-shadow-color', 'text-shadow')(match, ctx) ?? { 'text-shadow': h.bracket.cssvar.global(s) }
   262→  }, {
   263→    autocomplete: [
   264→      'text-shadow-$textShadow',
   265→      'text-shadow(-color)?-$colors',
   266→      'text-shadow(-color)?-(op|opacity)-<percent>',
   267→    ],
   268→  }],
   269→
   270→  // colors
   271→  [/^text-shadow-color-(.+)$/, colorResolver('--un-text-shadow-color', 'text-shadow'), { autocomplete: 'text-shadow-color-$colors' }],
   272→  [/^text-shadow-color-op(?:acity)?-?(.+)$/, ([, opacity]) => ({ '--un-text-shadow-opacity': h.bracket.percent.cssvar(opacity) }), { autocomplete: 'text-shadow-color-(op|opacity)-<percent>' }],
   273→]
   274→
   275→const fontVariantNumericProperties = [
   276→  defineProperty('--un-ordinal'),
   277→  defineProperty('--un-slashed-zero'),
   278→  defineProperty('--un-numeric-figure'),
   279→  defineProperty('--un-numeric-spacing'),
   280→  defineProperty('--un-numeric-fraction'),
   281→]
   282→const baseFontVariantNumeric = {
   283→  'font-variant-numeric': 'var(--un-ordinal,) var(--un-slashed-zero,) var(--un-numeric-figure,) var(--un-numeric-spacing,) var(--un-numeric-fraction,)',
   284→}
   285→
   286→export const fontVariantNumeric: Rule<Theme>[] = [
   287→  ['ordinal', [{ '--un-ordinal': 'ordinal', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   288→  ['slashed-zero', [{ '--un-slashed-zero': 'slashed-zero', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   289→  ['lining-nums', [{ '--un-numeric-figure': 'lining-nums', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   290→  ['oldstyle-nums', [{ '--un-numeric-figure': 'oldstyle-nums', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   291→  ['proportional-nums', [{ '--un-numeric-spacing': 'proportional-nums', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   292→  ['tabular-nums', [{ '--un-numeric-spacing': 'tabular-nums', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   293→  ['diagonal-fractions', [{ '--un-numeric-fraction': 'diagonal-fractions', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   294→  ['stacked-fractions', [{ '--un-numeric-fraction': 'stacked-fractions', ...baseFontVariantNumeric }, ...fontVariantNumericProperties]],
   295→  ['normal-nums', [{ 'font-variant-numeric': 'normal' }]],
   296→]
   297→
   298→function handleText([, s = 'base']: string[], { theme }: RuleContext<Theme>): CSSObject | undefined {
   299→  const split = splitShorthand(s, 'length')
   300→  if (!split)
   301→    return
   302→
   303→  const [size, leading] = split
   304→
   305→  const sizePairs = theme.text?.[size]
   306→  let lineHeight
   307→
   308→  if (leading) {
   309→    if (theme.leading?.[leading]) {
   310→      themeTracking(`leading`, leading)
   311→      lineHeight = generateThemeVariable('leading', leading)
   312→    }
   313→    else {
   314→      lineHeight = h.bracket.cssvar.global.rem(leading)
   315→    }
   316→  }
   317→
   318→  if (sizePairs) {
   319→    themeTracking(`text`, [size, 'fontSize'])
   320→    themeTracking(`text`, [size, 'lineHeight'])
   321→    if (sizePairs.letterSpacing) {
   322→      themeTracking(`text`, [size, 'letterSpacing'])
   323→    }
   324→
   325→    return {
   326→      'font-size': generateThemeVariable('text', [size, 'fontSize']),
   327→      'line-height': lineHeight ?? `var(--un-leading, ${generateThemeVariable('text', [size, 'lineHeight'])})`,
   328→      'letter-spacing': sizePairs.letterSpacing ? generateThemeVariable('text', [size, 'letterSpacing']) : undefined,
   329→    }
   330→  }
   331→
   332→  const fontSize = h.bracketOfLength.rem(size)
   333→  if (lineHeight && fontSize) {
   334→    return {
   335→      'font-size': fontSize,
   336→      'line-height': lineHeight,
   337→    }
   338→  }
   339→
   340→  return { 'font-size': h.bracketOfLength.rem(s) }
   341→}
   342→
   343→function handleSize([, s]: string[], { theme }: RuleContext<Theme>): CSSObject | undefined {
   344→  if (theme.text?.[s] != null) {
   345→    themeTracking(`text`, [s, 'fontSize'])
   346→    themeTracking(`text`, [s, 'lineHeight'])
   347→    return {
   348→      'font-size': generateThemeVariable('text', [s, 'fontSize']),
   349→      'line-height': `var(--un-leading, ${generateThemeVariable('text', [s, 'lineHeight'])})`,
   350→    }
   351→  }
   352→  else {
   353→    const d = h.bracket.cssvar.global.rem(s)
   354→    if (d)
   355→      return { 'font-size': d }
   356→  }
   357→}
   358→
   359→function handlerColorOrSize(match: RegExpMatchArray, ctx: RuleContext<Theme>): CSSObject | (CSSValueInput | string)[] | undefined {
   360→  if (isCSSMathFn(h.bracket(match[1])))
   361→    return handleSize(match, ctx)
   362→  return colorResolver('color', 'text')(match, ctx)
   363→}
   364→
   365→export function splitShorthand(body: string, type: string) {
   366→  const [front, rest] = getStringComponent(body, '[', ']', ['/', ':']) ?? []
   367→
   368→  if (front != null) {
   369→    const match = (front.match(bracketTypeRe) ?? [])[1]
   370→
   371→    if (match == null || match === type)
   372→      return [front, rest]
   373→  }
   374→}

     2→title: Wind4 preset
     3→description: The Tailwind4 CSS compact preset for UnoCSS (@unocss/preset-wind4).
     4→outline: deep
     5→---
     6→
     7→# Wind4 preset
     8→
     9→The Tailwind4 CSS compact preset for UnoCSS. It's compatible with all features of PresetWind3 and enhances it further.
    10→
    11→[Source Code](https://github.com/unocss/unocss/tree/main/packages-presets/preset-wind4)
    12→
    13→::: tip
    14→You can spend a little time reading this document to understand the changes
    15→:::
    16→
    17→## Installation
    18→
    19→::: code-group
    20→
    21→```bash [pnpm]
    22→pnpm add -D @unocss/preset-wind4
    23→```
    24→
    25→```bash [yarn]
    26→yarn add -D @unocss/preset-wind4
    27→```
    28→
    29→```bash [npm]
    30→npm install -D @unocss/preset-wind4
    31→```
    32→
    33→```bash [bun]
    34→bun add -D @unocss/preset-wind4
    35→```
    36→
    37→:::
    38→
    39→```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
    40→import presetWind4 from '@unocss/preset-wind4'
    41→import { defineConfig } from 'unocss'
    42→
    43→export default defineConfig({
    44→  presets: [
    45→    presetWind4(),
    46→    //  ^?
    47→  ],
    48→})
    49→```
    50→
    51→## Compatibility
    52→
    53→Refer to [Tailwind Compatibility](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/compatibility) to learn about browser support and compatibility.
    54→
    55→## Theme
    56→
    57→`PresetWind4`'s theme is almost identical to `PresetWind3`'s theme, but some theme keys have been adjusted.
    58→
    59→::: warning
    60→Please note when switching to PresetWind4, refer to the table below to check your theme key configuration and make appropriate adjustments.
    61→:::
    62→
    63→|                                            PresetWind3                                            |                          PresetWind4                          |
    64→| :-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: | :-----------------------------------------------------------: |
    65→|                                           `fontFamily`                                            |                            `font`                             |
    66→|                                            `fontSize`                                             |            Moved to `fontSize` property in `text`             |
    67→|                                           `lineHeight`                                            |   Moved to `lineHeight` property in `text` or use `leading`   |
    68→|                                          `letterSpacing`                                          | Moved to `letterSpacing` property in `text` or use `tracking` |
    69→|                                          `borderRadius`                                           |                           `radius`                            |
    70→|                                             `easing`                                              |                            `ease`                             |
    71→|                                           `breakpoints`                                           |                         `breakpoint`                          |
    72→|                                       `verticalBreakpoints`                                       |                     `verticalBreakpoint`                      |
    73→|                                            `boxShadow`                                            |                           `shadow`                            |
    74→|                                                 -                                                 |                         `insetShadow`                         |
    75→|     Size properties like `width`, `height`, `maxWidth`, `maxHeight`, `minWidth`, `minHeight`      |                   Unified to use `spacing`                    |
    76→|                                       `transitionProperty`                                        |                          `property`                           |
    77→| `gridAutoColumn`, `gridAutoRow`, `gridColumn`, `gridRow`, `gridTemplateColumn`, `gridTemplateRow` |                               -                               |
    78→|                                       `container.maxWidth`                                        |                     `containers.maxWidth`                     |
    79→|                                                 -                                                 |                          `defaults`                           |
    80→
    81→### `Theme.defaults`
    82→
    83→`Theme.defaults` is a global default theme configuration that will be applied to `reset` styles or used as default values for certain rules.
    84→
    85→Below are the default values for `Theme.defaults`, which you can override in your theme configuration.
    86→
    87→<details>
    88→<summary>Click to view default values</summary>
    89→
    90→```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
    91→import type { Theme } from '@unocss/preset-wind4/theme'
    92→
    93→export const defaults: Theme['default'] = {
    94→  transition: {
    95→    duration: '150ms',
    96→    timingFunction: 'cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)',
    97→  },
    98→  font: {
    99→    family: 'var(--font-sans)',
   100→    featureSettings: 'var(--font-sans--font-feature-settings)',
   101→    variationSettings: 'var(--font-sans--font-variation-settings)',
   102→  },
   103→  monoFont: {
   104→    family: 'var(--font-mono)',
   105→    featureSettings: 'var(--font-mono--font-feature-settings)',
   106→    variationSettings: 'var(--font-mono--font-variation-settings)',
   107→  },
   108→}
   109→```
   110→
   111→</details>
   112→
   113→## Options
   114→
   115→PresetWind4's basic configuration is similar to [PresetWind3](/presets/wind3#options), with the following important changes.
   116→
   117→### Preflights
   118→
   119→We have added the `preflights` configuration option in `PresetWind4` to control whether to enable preset styles.
   120→
   121→#### Reset
   122→
   123→In PresetWind4, we align the reset styles with tailwind4 and integrate them internally. You don't need to install any additional CSS reset package like `@unocss/reset` or `normalize.css`.
   124→
   125→```ts [main.ts]
   126→import '@unocss/reset/tailwind.css' // [!code --]
   127→import '@unocss/reset/tailwind-compat.css' // [!code --]
   128→```
   129→
   130→You only need to control whether to enable reset styles through a switch:
   131→
   132→```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
   133→import presetWind4 from '@unocss/preset-wind4'
   134→import { defineConfig } from 'unocss'
   135→
   136→export default defineConfig({
   137→  presets: [
   138→    presetWind4({
   139→      preflights: { // [!code ++]
   140→        reset: true, // [!code ++]
   141→      } // [!code ++]
   142→    }),
   143→  ],
   144→})
   145→```
   146→
   147→#### Theme
   148→
   149→Choose how to generate theme CSS variables.
   150→
   151→##### Mode
   152→
   153→The UnoCSS engine with `presetWind4` installed will automatically collect dependencies on the theme when parsing utilities and generate CSS variables at the end.
   154→
   155→- `true`: Generate theme keys fully.
   156→- `false`: Disable theme keys. (Not recommended ⚠️)
   157→- `'on-demand'`: Generate theme keys only when used. -> ✅ **(By default)**
   158→
   159→```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
   160→import { defineConfig, presetWind4 } from 'unocss'
   161→
   162→export default defineConfig({
   163→  presets: [
   164→    presetWind4({
   165→      preflights: { // [!code ++]
   166→        theme: true, // [!code ++]
   167→      }, // [!code ++]
   168→    }),
   169→  ],
   170→})
   171→```
   172→
   173→##### Process
   174→
   175→And you can further control the output of your theme variables. For example, if you want to convert `rem` to `px` for theme variables, we provide the `createRemToPxProcessor` function to process your theme variables.
   176→
   177→```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
   178→import { createRemToPxProcessor } from '@unocss/preset-wind4/utils' // [!code ++]
   179→import { defineConfig, presetWind4 } from 'unocss'
   180→
   181→export default defineConfig({
   182→  presets: [
   183→    presetWind4({
   184→      preflights: { // [!code ++]
   185→        theme: { // [!code ++]
   186→          mode: 'on-demand', // Default by 'on-demand' // [!code ++]
   187→          process: createRemToPxProcessor(), // [!code ++]
   188→        } // [!code ++]
   189→      }, // [!code ++]
   190→    }),
   191→  ],
   192→})
   193→```
   194→
   195→By the way, if you want to use the `presetRemToPx` preset to convert `rem` to `px`, you no longer need to import this preset separately as `presetWind4` provides this functionality internally.
   196→
   197→```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
   198→import { createRemToPxProcessor } from '@unocss/preset-wind4/utils' // [!code ++]
   199→import { defineConfig, presetWind4 } from 'unocss'
   200→
   201→export default defineConfig({
   202→  presets: [
   203→    presetWind4({
   204→      preflights: { // [!code ++]
   205→        theme: { // [!code ++]
   206→          process: createRemToPxProcessor(), // [!code ++]
   207→        } // [!code ++]
   208→      }, // [!code ++]
   209→    }),
   210→  ],
   211→  postprocess: [createRemToPxProcessor()], // [!code ++]
   212→})
   213→```
   214→
   215→## Generated CSS
   216→
   217→In the output of PresetWind4, three new layers have been added: `base`, `theme`, and `properties`.
   218→
   219→|  Layer Name  |              Description              | order |
   220→| :----------: | :-----------------------------------: | :---: |
   221→| `properties` | CSS properties defined by `@property` | -200  |
   222→|   `theme`    |      Theme-related CSS variables      | -150  |
   223→|    `base`    |      Base preflight/reset styles      | -100  |
   224→
   225→### `properties` Layer
   226→
   227→We have used `@property` to define CSS properties in many rules to achieve better performance and smaller size.
   228→
   229→For example, commonly used utilities like `text-op-xx`, `bg-op-xx`, and so on.
   230→
   231→```css
   232→@property --un-text-opacity {
   233→  syntax: '<percentage>';
   234→  inherits: false;
   235→  initial-value: 100%;
   236→}
   237→```
   238→
   239→### `theme` Layer
   240→

---
title: Wind4 preset
description: The Tailwind4 CSS compact preset for UnoCSS (@unocss/preset-wind4).
outline: deep
---

# Wind4 preset

The Tailwind4 CSS compact preset for UnoCSS. It's compatible with all features of PresetWind3 and enhances it further.

[Source Code](https://github.com/unocss/unocss/tree/main/packages-presets/preset-wind4)

::: tip
You can spend a little time reading this document to understand the changes
:::

## Installation

::: code-group

```bash [pnpm]
pnpm add -D @unocss/preset-wind4
```

```bash [yarn]
yarn add -D @unocss/preset-wind4
```

```bash [npm]
npm install -D @unocss/preset-wind4
```

```bash [bun]
bun add -D @unocss/preset-wind4
```

:::

```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
import presetWind4 from '@unocss/preset-wind4'
import { defineConfig } from 'unocss'

export default defineConfig({
  presets: [
    presetWind4(),
    //  ^?
  ],
})
```

## Compatibility

Refer to [Tailwind Compatibility](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/compatibility) to learn about browser support and compatibility.

## Theme

`PresetWind4`'s theme is almost identical to `PresetWind3`'s theme, but some theme keys have been adjusted.

::: warning
Please note when switching to PresetWind4, refer to the table below to check your theme key configuration and make appropriate adjustments.
:::

|                                            PresetWind3                                            |                          PresetWind4                          |
| :-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: | :-----------------------------------------------------------: |
|                                           `fontFamily`                                            |                            `font`                             |
|                                            `fontSize`                                             |            Moved to `fontSize` property in `text`             |
|                                           `lineHeight`                                            |   Moved to `lineHeight` property in `text` or use `leading`   |
|                                          `letterSpacing`                                          | Moved to `letterSpacing` property in `text` or use `tracking` |
|                                          `borderRadius`                                           |                           `radius`                            |
|                                             `easing`                                              |                            `ease`                             |
|                                           `breakpoints`                                           |                         `breakpoint`                          |
|                                       `verticalBreakpoints`                                       |                     `verticalBreakpoint`                      |
|                                            `boxShadow`                                            |                           `shadow`                            |
|                                                 -                                                 |                         `insetShadow`                         |
|     Size properties like `width`, `height`, `maxWidth`, `maxHeight`, `minWidth`, `minHeight`      |                   Unified to use `spacing`                    |
|                                       `transitionProperty`                                        |                          `property`                           |
| `gridAutoColumn`, `gridAutoRow`, `gridColumn`, `gridRow`, `gridTemplateColumn`, `gridTemplateRow` |                               -                               |
|                                       `container.maxWidth`                                        |                     `containers.maxWidth`                     |
|                                                 -                                                 |                          `defaults`                           |

### `Theme.defaults`

`Theme.defaults` is a global default theme configuration that will be applied to `reset` styles or used as default values for certain rules.

Below are the default values for `Theme.defaults`, which you can override in your theme configuration.

<details>
<summary>Click to view default values</summary>

```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
import type { Theme } from '@unocss/preset-wind4/theme'

export const defaults: Theme['default'] = {
  transition: {
    duration: '150ms',
    timingFunction: 'cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)',
  },
  font: {
    family: 'var(--font-sans)',
    featureSettings: 'var(--font-sans--font-feature-settings)',
    variationSettings: 'var(--font-sans--font-variation-settings)',
  },
  monoFont: {
    family: 'var(--font-mono)',
    featureSettings: 'var(--font-mono--font-feature-settings)',
    variationSettings: 'var(--font-mono--font-variation-settings)',
  },
}
```

</details>

## Options

PresetWind4's basic configuration is similar to [PresetWind3](/presets/wind3#options), with the following important changes.

### Preflights

We have added the `preflights` configuration option in `PresetWind4` to control whether to enable preset styles.

#### Reset

In PresetWind4, we align the reset styles with tailwind4 and integrate them internally. You don't need to install any additional CSS reset package like `@unocss/reset` or `normalize.css`.

```ts [main.ts]
import '@unocss/reset/tailwind.css' // [!code --]
import '@unocss/reset/tailwind-compat.css' // [!code --]
```

You only need to control whether to enable reset styles through a switch:

```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
import presetWind4 from '@unocss/preset-wind4'
import { defineConfig } from 'unocss'

export default defineConfig({
  presets: [
    presetWind4({
      preflights: { // [!code ++]
        reset: true, // [!code ++]
      } // [!code ++]
    }),
  ],
})
```

#### Theme

Choose how to generate theme CSS variables.

##### Mode

The UnoCSS engine with `presetWind4` installed will automatically collect dependencies on the theme when parsing utilities and generate CSS variables at the end.

- `true`: Generate theme keys fully.
- `false`: Disable theme keys. (Not recommended ⚠️)
- `'on-demand'`: Generate theme keys only when used. -> ✅ **(By default)**

```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
import { defineConfig, presetWind4 } from 'unocss'

export default defineConfig({
  presets: [
    presetWind4({
      preflights: { // [!code ++]
        theme: true, // [!code ++]
      }, // [!code ++]
    }),
  ],
})
```

##### Process

And you can further control the output of your theme variables. For example, if you want to convert `rem` to `px` for theme variables, we provide the `createRemToPxProcessor` function to process your theme variables.

```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
import { createRemToPxProcessor } from '@unocss/preset-wind4/utils' // [!code ++]
import { defineConfig, presetWind4 } from 'unocss'

export default defineConfig({
  presets: [
    presetWind4({
      preflights: { // [!code ++]
        theme: { // [!code ++]
          mode: 'on-demand', // Default by 'on-demand' // [!code ++]
          process: createRemToPxProcessor(), // [!code ++]
        } // [!code ++]
      }, // [!code ++]
    }),
  ],
})
```

By the way, if you want to use the `presetRemToPx` preset to convert `rem` to `px`, you no longer need to import this preset separately as `presetWind4` provides this functionality internally.

```ts twoslash [uno.config.ts]
import { createRemToPxProcessor } from '@unocss/preset-wind4/utils' // [!code ++]
import { defineConfig, presetWind4 } from 'unocss'

export default defineConfig({
  presets: [
    presetWind4({
      preflights: { // [!code ++]
        theme: { // [!code ++]
          process: createRemToPxProcessor(), // [!code ++]
        } // [!code ++]
      }, // [!code ++]
    }),
  ],
  postprocess: [createRemToPxProcessor()], // [!code ++]
})
```

## Generated CSS

In the output of PresetWind4, three new layers have been added: `base`, `theme`, and `properties`.

|  Layer Name  |              Description              | order |
| :----------: | :-----------------------------------: | :---: |
| `properties` | CSS properties defined by `@property` | -200  |
|   `theme`    |      Theme-related CSS variables      | -150  |
|    `base`    |      Base preflight/reset styles      | -100  |

### `properties` Layer

We have used `@property` to define CSS properties in many rules to achieve better performance and smaller size.

For example, commonly used utilities like `text-op-xx`, `bg-op-xx`, and so on.

```css
@property --un-text-opacity {
  syntax: '<percentage>';
  inherits: false;
  initial-value: 100%;
}
```

### `theme` Layer

We've placed theme-related CSS variables in the `theme` layer to make it easier for you to override and use directly.
It can be comprehensive or on-demand. It always comes from your theme configuration.

::: info
The generated key names may not be exactly the same as `Tailwind4`. We try to avoid making significant changes to the key names in the theme to respect users migrating from `presetWind3`.
You can also customize the output you want in the [Preflights Theme Process](#process).
:::

```css
:root,
:host {
  --spacing: 0.25rem;
  --font-sans: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial,
    'Noto Sans', sans-serif, 'Apple Color Emoji', 'Segoe UI Emoji', 'Segoe UI Symbol', 'Noto Color Emoji';
  --font-serif: ui-serif, Georgia, Cambria, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;
  --font-mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', 'Courier New', monospace;
  --colors-black: #000;
  --colors-white: #fff;
  /* ... */
}
```

## Compatibility with Other Presets

`PresetWind4` enhances and is compatible with `PresetWind3`. Since other packages were originally developed for `PresetWind3`, some issues may arise when using them together. Known issues include:

### presetRemToPx

`presetRemToPx` is no longer needed in `PresetWind4` as it is already included internally. You can remove it from your configuration.

Refer to the [`process`](#process) option in Options.

### presetLegacyCompat

In `presetWind4`, we use the `oklch` color model to support better color contrast and color perception. Therefore, it is not compatible with `presetLegacyCompat` and is **not recommended** for use together.

Please refer to the [Compatibility](#compatibility) section for more information.

   337→export function colorableShadows(shadows: string | string[], colorVar: string) {
   338→  const colored = []
   339→  shadows = toArray(shadows)
   340→  for (let i = 0; i < shadows.length; i++) {
   341→    // shadow values are between 3 to 6 terms including color
   342→    const components = getStringComponents(shadows[i], ' ', 6)
   343→    if (!components || components.length < 3)
   344→      return shadows
   345→
   346→    let isInset = false
   347→    const pos = components.indexOf('inset')
   348→    if (pos !== -1) {
   349→      components.splice(pos, 1)
   350→      isInset = true
   351→    }
   352→
   353→    let colorVarValue = ''
   354→    const lastComp = components.at(-1)
   355→    if (parseCssColor(components.at(0))) {
   356→      const color = parseCssColor(components.shift())
   357→      if (color)
   358→        colorVarValue = `, ${colorToString(color)}`
   359→    }
   360→    else if (parseCssColor(lastComp)) {
   361→      const color = parseCssColor(components.pop())
   362→      if (color)
   363→        colorVarValue = `, ${colorToString(color)}`
   364→    }
   365→    else if (lastComp && cssVarFnRE.test(lastComp)) {
   366→      const color = components.pop()!
   367→      colorVarValue = `, ${color}`
   368→    }
   369→
   370→    colored.push(`${isInset ? 'inset ' : ''}${components.join(' ')} var(${colorVar}${colorVarValue})`)
   371→  }
   372→
   373→  return colored
   374→}

   386→export function resolveBreakpoints({ theme, generator }: Readonly<VariantContext<Theme>>, key: 'breakpoint' | 'verticalBreakpoint' = 'breakpoint') {
   387→  const breakpoints: Record<string, string> | undefined = (generator?.userConfig?.theme as any)?.[key] || theme[key]

   389→  if (!breakpoints)

   391→
   392→  if (resolvedBreakpoints.has(theme))

   394→
   395→  const resolved = Object.entries(breakpoints)

   399→  resolvedBreakpoints.set(theme, resolved)

content.text: Fixed: when `presetWind4({ important: '#__nuxt' })` is used, we now **skip scoping the `properties` layer** in the `important` postprocessor. This prevents invalid output like `#__nuxt :is(@property --un-text-opacity) { ... }`, which was causing the `@property --un-text-opacity` registration to be dropped and `--un-text-opacity` to appear undefined in devtools. - Updated: `packages-presets/preset-wind4/src/postprocess/important.ts` - Wrote a regression test to reproduce, verified it fails pre-fix and passes post-fix, then **deleted the test** as requested. - Re-ran `pnpm test test/preset-wind4.test.ts` to confirm the suite still passes.
